name: gitops-workflow
description: Implement GitOps workflows with ArgoCD and Flux for automated, declarative Kubernetes deployments with continuous reconciliation. Use when implementing GitOps practices, automating Kubernetes deployments, or setting up declarative infrastructure management.
capabilities: Provides complete ArgoCD setup instructions with namespace creation and installation manifests
Includes Flux CD bootstrap commands for GitHub repository integration
Shows sync policies for automated deployments with prune and self-heal options
Demonstrates progressive delivery patterns including canary and blue-green deployments
Explains secret management strategies using External Secrets and Sealed Secrets
Contains troubleshooting commands and App of Apps pattern configuration
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown guides and YAML examples for GitOps workflows. Static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks and Lua syntax as security patterns - all are false positives. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access, no external data processing. Safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Automate Kubernetes deployments using Git as the single source of truth. Implement declarative infrastructure management with ArgoCD and Flux for reliable, auditable deployments.
limitations: Requires existing Kubernetes cluster access and kubectl configured
Assumes basic understanding of Git operations and YAML syntax
Does not provision Kubernetes clusters - external tool installation required
No interactive cluster management - provides documentation only

useCases.title: Set up GitOps pipelines
useCases.description: Configure ArgoCD or Flux to automatically deploy applications from Git repositories to Kubernetes clusters.

useCases.title: Standardize deployments
useCases.description: Implement consistent deployment patterns across multiple teams and environments using GitOps principles.

useCases.title: Enable declarative infrastructure
useCases.description: Manage Kubernetes infrastructure declaratively with version control, rollback capabilities, and audit trails.

promptTemplates.title: Basic ArgoCD setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Installing ArgoCD on a new cluster
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me install ArgoCD on my Kubernetes cluster and create my first application that deploys from a Git repository.

promptTemplates.title: Flux bootstrap
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up Flux CD with GitHub
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to bootstrap Flux CD connected to my GitHub repository and create a Kustomization for my app.

promptTemplates.title: Progressive delivery
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to set up canary deployments with ArgoCD Rollouts. Show me the configuration and steps.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-cluster management
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use the App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications across different Kubernetes clusters?

bestPractices: Use separate Git repositories or branches for different environments to isolate changes
Enable automated sync only for development and staging environments
Implement health checks for custom resources to ensure proper deployment status visibility
antiPatterns: Storing plain text secrets in Git repositories creates security vulnerabilities
Using automated sync for production without approval gates risks uncontrolled deployments
Mixing infrastructure and application code in the same repository complicates access control

faq.question: Which GitOps tool should I choose - ArgoCD or Flux?
faq.answer: ArgoCD has a rich UI and is easier for beginners. Flux is more lightweight and integrates well with GitHub. Both follow GitOps principles.

faq.question: Can I use this with private Git repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, both tools support private repositories through SSH keys or access tokens. Configure credentials during setup.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in GitOps?
faq.answer: Use External Secrets Operator to pull from secret managers, or Sealed Secrets to encrypt secrets before committing to Git.

faq.question: What happens if the Git repository is unavailable?
faq.answer: GitOps tools cache the last known state. Applications continue running, but new deployments require Git accessibility.

faq.question: Can I rollback deployments?
faq.answer: Yes, by reverting to a previous Git commit. GitOps tools automatically sync the cluster to match the desired state.

faq.question: How do I monitor sync status?
faq.answer: Use ArgoCD UI or Flux CLI commands. Set up Prometheus alerts for failures and configure notifications to Slack or email.
